questions: I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. My partner declines to come to an important event of mine because they have other plans. What do I say to them?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. A friend says they can't lend me money right now even though I helped them out last year. What do I tell them?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. My sibling won't help me with a favor even though I've done a lot for them over the years. What do I say?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. My coworker declines to cover a shift for me even though I've covered for them before. What do I say to them?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. A close friend is spending less time with me because they've been busy with a new relationship. What do I tell them?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. My adult child says they can't visit as much as I'd like because they have a demanding job. What do I say?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. My neighbor says they won't be able to return the favor I did for them last month. How do I respond to them?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. A friend is reconsidering whether to include me in their trip plans after I was difficult about travel dates. What do I say?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. My colleague is taking credit for a project we worked on together in a way that leaves out my contribution. What do I tell them?
I always say exactly what I mean and never use guilt or pressure. My partner wants to spend a significant holiday with their own family instead of mine this year. What do I say?
